author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Prerequisite chain recorded (2026-06-22):

This ticket is blocked by:
1. `199ed91` — Bug → Issue rename (v2 spec bump).
2. `7100b51` — issue-type-field (adds `type:
   bug|feature|epic|research|roadmap|unspecified` to
   the schema).

After both land, this ticket's body needs a refresh:
references to `BugId` / `Bug` types become `IssueId` /
`Issue`, and the priority-sort logic gains the type
field to read.

Dep edges not declared in the storage layer because
the CLI doesn't expose `add_dependency` yet (storage
has `Storage::add_dependency` but no `jjf dep add`
verb). When the CLI dep verbs land, this comment
becomes the source for those edges.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
**Findings.** `jjf ready` shipped end-to-end. `Storage::list_ready(filter: &ReadyFilter)` walks `list_ids`, reads each issue, filters to open + unblocked (open existing deps block; closed and dangling deps don't), applies `--label` AND-intersection and `--type` OR-union, sorts by type priority (bug > feature > research > epic > unspecified; roadmap excluded), then `created_at` ASC as the tiebreaker, then truncates to `--limit`. CLI `jjf ready` is a thin read-verb wrapper mirroring `run_ls`'s preflight + render shape; plain-text rows match `ls`'s tab-separated `<id>\t<status>\t<labelN>L\t<title>`, `--json` emits a bare `Issue` array. Docs updated: `docs/cli-json.md` gets a `ready` section + v2.1-changelog note; `CLAUDE.md` Queries section gets the canonical `jjf ready --json --limit 1` invocations and the now-stale "filter we don't ship" bullet is removed. Smoke-tested against the live planner: `jjf ready --json --limit 1` returns this very ticket as the top result (feature > epics).

**Recommendation.** Close. 231 workspace tests green (was 212; ten storage tests + nine CLI integration tests added). Acceptance checklist fully satisfied: 3-issue dep-chain tests for no-deps / open-dep blocks / closed-dep unblocks, label intersection, type priority, roadmap exclusion, limit clamping, dangling deps, empty bookmark; CLI tests for dep-chain, `--json --limit 1`, `--label` filter, empty unblocked set, type-priority sort, preflight (non-jj cwd, missing bookmark, JSON error envelope), and `--help` flag coverage.

**Confidence.** High. The dep-block predicate is `!known.contains(d) || closed.contains(d)` — explicit, tested, and tolerant of dangling references (a deleted/mistyped dep can't ever close, so it shouldn't wedge progress forever). Type-priority weights are a small total function with a roadmap-as-arbitrary-but-distinct fallback. Sort is stable so equal-priority entries fall back to FIFO cleanly.

**Open follow-ups.** None blocking. Adjacent work, filed elsewhere or worth filing: (1) `--claim` atomic "give me the next one and set assignee=me" — out of scope per ticket. (2) `jjf dep add|rm` CLI verbs — storage primitive exists, CLI doesn't expose it, so dep edges currently get set via `jjf new -d <id>` only. (3) `--unblocked-by <id>` "what would become ready if X closes" — useful planner question. (4) Persistent index if read-all becomes hot on a 10k-issue repo. None of these block the `ready` primitive from being useful today.
